steps.title: Find the adjacent angle
steps.content:
Angle $b$ is $122^\circ$, so its adjacent angle on line $q$ is supplementary.
$$180^\circ-122^\circ=58^\circ$$

steps.title: Use the triangle angle
steps.content:
The angle labeled $a$ is $43^\circ$. The exterior obtuse angle made with the parallel line is the sum of the two remote interior angles.
$$43^\circ+58^\circ=101^\circ$$

steps.title: Split the marked angle
steps.content:
The diagram marks that obtuse angle as two equal angles, each $w$.
$$2w=101$$
Divide by $2$. The answer is 101/2.
